input.background: Let us submit the cholesterol illustrative data to an independent $t$ test. Recall that $n_1=12, \bar{x}_1=6.192$ and $s_1=0.3919$. In group $2, n_2=7, \bar{x}_2=5.414$ and $s_2=0.6492$. We've established that $\bar{x}_1-\bar{x}_2=0.777(\mathrm{mmol} / \mathrm{L})$ and $S E_{\bar{x}_1-\bar{x}_2}=0.2702$. The degrees of freedom are either $d f_{\text {conserv }}=6$ or $d f_{\text {Welch }}=8.61$.
input.question: A. Hypotheses. $H_0: \mu_1=\mu_2$ against $H_a: \mu_1>\mu_2$ (one-sided alterative) or $H_a: \mu_1 \neq \mu_2$ (two-sided).

sd1.description: Standard deviation of cholesterol levels in group 1

sd2.description: Standard deviation of cholesterol levels in group 2

mean_diff.description: Difference in mean cholesterol levels between group 1 and group 2

se_diff.description: Standard error of the difference in mean cholesterol levels

df_conserv.description: Degrees of freedom using conservative method

df_welch.description: Degrees of freedom using Welch's method

t_table.description: Critical values from the t-distribution table for different degrees of freedom and probabilities

hypothesis.description: Null and alternative hypotheses for the t-test
